title: scalia capillaris
intro: Scalia capillaris, also known as sweet woodruff, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white, star-shaped flowers and sweetly scented leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a carminative and antispasmodic, to ease digestive issues and relieve muscle cramps. Scalia capillaris has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Scalia capillaris include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
